translations.code.ai.reopenToRetry: Reopen this tab to retry — no auto-retry to avoid token spend.
Reopen this tab to retry — no auto-retry to avoid token spend.
translations.code.app.subtitle: AI-Powered Codebase Intelligence
translations.code.app.title: Flyto2 Code
translations.code.common.loadError: Failed to load data. Please try again.
translations.code.dashboard.connectForPRs: Connect GitHub in Settings to see PR activity
translations.code.dashboard.connectToStart: Go to Settings to connect your repositories
translations.code.dashboard.runScanToSee: Run a scan to see security findings
translations.code.dashboard.scanAllToStart: Scan all repos to populate dashboard
translations.code.dast.clickjackingDesc: Without X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors, attackers can embed your site in an iframe for clickjacking attacks.
translations.code.dast.cookieNoEncryptDesc: A cookie is set without the 'secure' flag, meaning it can be intercepted over HTTP connections.
translations.code.dast.cookieNoHttpDesc: A cookie without HttpOnly can be accessed by JavaScript, enabling session theft via XSS.
translations.code.dast.cookiePoisonDesc: URL parameters can set cookie values, which may be exploitable for session manipulation.
translations.code.dast.cookieSubdomainDesc: Authentication cookies scoped to parent domain can be accessed by any subdomain, increasing attack surface.
translations.code.dast.cspEvalDesc: CSP does not block eval(), setTimeout() and other functions that can execute unknown code, enabling code injection.
translations.code.dast.cspFallbackDesc: CSP does not define a fallback for a directive, causing the browser to treat that content type as unrestricted.
translations.code.dast.cspInlineCssDesc: CSP allows inline CSS, which can aid social engineering attacks by injecting misleading visual elements.
translations.code.dast.cspInlineJsDesc: CSP is configured but allows inline JavaScript, which is a common XSS attack vector.
translations.code.dast.cspNotSet: Content Security Policy (CSP) header not set
translations.code.dast.cspNotSetDesc: CSP is the first line of defense against XSS and data injection attacks. It controls which content sources are allowed to load and execute.
translations.code.dast.dirBrowsingDesc: Directory listings are enabled, potentially revealing hidden scripts, backup files, and sensitive information.
translations.code.dast.envExposedDesc: Environment files expose infrastructure credentials, API keys, or other sensitive configuration information.
translations.code.dast.graphqlIntrospectDesc: GraphQL introspection reveals the entire API schema including all types, queries, and mutations.
translations.code.dast.aiRunningHint: Hard rules score your attack surface. AI finds patterns humans miss.
translations.code.dast.heartbleedDesc: OpenSSL Heartbeat Extension vulnerability allows remote attackers to read process memory, potentially disclosing sensitive information.
translations.code.dast.hstsNotSet: TLS not enforced with valid HSTS header
translations.code.dast.hstsNotSetDesc: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) forces browsers to use HTTPS only. Without it, users are vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks via protocol downgrade.
translations.code.dast.httpOnlyDesc: The site responds on HTTP without redirecting to HTTPS, exposing traffic to interception and modification.
translations.code.dast.jwtInvalidDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens with invalid signatures, allowing attackers to modify token payload without detection.
translations.code.dast.jwtNoneDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens signed with the 'none' algorithm, bypassing signature verification entirely.
translations.code.dast.jwtSelfSignedDesc: The server accepts JWT tokens with self-signed JWK keys, allowing attackers to impersonate legitimate users.
translations.code.dast.jwtWeakDesc: The JWT token returned by your server uses a weak or known compromised secret, allowing attackers to forge tokens for unauthorized access.
translations.code.dast.linkRepoHint: Link a repository to filter API routes
translations.code.dast.noDmarcDesc: Without DMARC, there is no policy to handle emails that fail SPF/DKIM checks, enabling phishing attacks using your domain.
translations.code.dast.noDnssecDesc: Without DNSSEC, DNS responses can be spoofed, potentially redirecting users to malicious servers.
translations.code.dast.noPageSpeed: No PageSpeed data yet. Run discovery to test.
translations.code.dast.noSniffDesc: Without 'nosniff', browsers may interpret files as a different MIME type, enabling XSS via MIME-type confusion.
translations.code.dast.noSpfDesc: Without SPF (Sender Policy Framework), attackers can send emails appearing to come from your domain (email spoofing).
translations.code.dast.notVerified: Not verified yet — run discovery to test
translations.code.dast.openPortDesc: A service port is publicly accessible that should typically be behind a firewall. Database, cache, and remote access ports exposed to the internet are high-risk attack vectors.
translations.code.dast.poweredByLeakDesc: The server exposes the framework/runtime it uses, helping attackers identify specific vulnerabilities
translations.code.dast.psHintA11y: Missing alt text, contrast issues, or ARIA labels.
translations.code.dast.psHintBP: Using deprecated APIs or insecure resources.
translations.code.dast.psHintPerf: Slow loading. Optimize images, lazy-load, reduce JS bundle.
translations.code.dast.psHintSEO: Missing meta tags, no sitemap, or poor mobile usability.
translations.code.dast.serverLeak: Server leaks info via 'Server' HTTP header
translations.code.dast.serverLeakDesc: The server is exposing its software identity via the Server header. This helps attackers identify known vulnerabilities
translations.code.dast.sessionCookieDesc: The session cookie is missing 'httpOnly' and 'secure' attributes, making it vulnerable to theft by third-party scripts.
translations.code.dast.sessionStorage: Session token found in browser storage
translations.code.dast.sessionStorageDesc: Session tokens stored in localStorage/sessionStorage have no protection against injected third-party scripts.
translations.code.dast.sourceCodeGitDesc: Git repository files are publicly accessible, potentially exposing source code and sensitive configuration.
translations.code.dast.sriMissingDesc: External scripts/styles loaded without integrity attributes can be tampered with if the CDN is compromised.
translations.code.dast.sslExpiryDesc: The TLS certificate is about to expire, which will break HTTPS connections and trigger browser warnings.
translations.code.dast.tabNabbingDesc: Links with target='_blank' without 'noopener noreferrer' allow the target page to take control of the opener.
translations.code.fixPlan.emptySub: Generate a week-bucketed remediation roadmap from the current findings.
translations.code.fixPlan.emptySubAuto: Auto-generated after each scan. Trigger a fresh scan to produce a new plan.
translations.code.fixPlan.title: Fix Plan
translations.code.gitlabCallback.exchangeFailed: Failed to exchange the authorization code for a token.
translations.code.gitlabCallback.invalidState: Invalid OAuth state. Please try connecting again.
translations.code.gitlabCallback.missingVerifier: PKCE verifier missing. Please restart the connection.
translations.code.issues.name: Name
translations.code.issues.skip_reason_manifest_not_fou: Package not found in any manifest
translations.code.issues.skip_reason_no_fix_version: No fix version is available yet
translations.code.login.resetSubtitle: We'll send you a reset link
translations.code.login.unexpectedError: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
translations.code.login.cantCreateAccount: Unable to create account. Please try signing in instead.
translations.code.login.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Please try again later
translations.code.login.weakPassword: Password must be at least 6 characters
translations.code.news.title: Security News
translations.code.onboarding.connectGithubDesc: Connect your GitHub account to get started
translations.code.pentest.addDomainFirst: Add a domain in 'Domains & API' first, then run penetration tests against it here.
translations.code.pentest.attackSurfaceDesc: Discover subdomains, DNS, exposed credentials, misconfigurations
translations.code.pentest.check.accessControlDesc: Identify unauthorized data access. Check cross-tenant data leakage, authorization bypass, IDOR (data exposure risk), and improper access control.
translations.code.pentest.check.authSessionDesc: Verify login security. Check for brute force vulnerabilities, missing/improper authentication, and cookie integrity issues.
translations.code.pentest.check.businessLogicDesc: AI-driven logic testing. Detect complex input validation errors and general logic flaws that static scanners cannot identify.
translations.code.pentest.check.clientSideDesc: Protect browser security. Scan for XSS, CSRF, open redirects, and web cache poisoning attacks.
translations.code.pentest.check.codeInjectionDesc: Detect if untrusted data executes system commands. Focus on Remote Code Execution (RCE) and OS command injection.
translations.code.pentest.check.deserializationDesc: Probe unsafe object handling. Target insecure deserialization and server-side template injection.
translations.code.pentest.check.fileMisconfigDesc: Scan filesystem risks including Local File Inclusion (LFI), unrestricted file upload, directory listing, path control, and error leakage.
translations.code.pentest.check.hardeningDesc: Identify missing or misconfigured defense controls including GraphQL hardening, CORS misconfiguration, TLS issues, and incorrect HTTP security headers.
translations.code.pentest.check.llmInjectionDesc: Ensure AI integrations are tamper-proof. Detect prompt injection, jailbreak, and system context leakage attempts.
translations.code.pentest.check.secretsCryptoDesc: Discover hardcoded credentials/passwords, weak encryption, JWT validation bypass, and sensitive data exposure.
translations.code.pentest.check.sqlInjectionDesc: Identify injection vulnerabilities in database queries. Covers SQL injection, NoSQL injection, XPath injection, and LDAP injection.
translations.code.pentest.check.ssrfDesc: Test if your server can be tricked into making requests to internal infrastructure or external systems.
translations.code.pentest.frontendDesc: Test CSP, cookies, exposed files, JWT, and other vulnerabilities
translations.code.pentest.graphqlDesc: Test GraphQL API against misconfigurations and attacks
translations.code.pentest.restApiDesc: Test against SQLi, SSRF, BOLA and other attacks
translations.code.pentest.selectTarget: Select a target to run tests
translations.code.pentest.stagingWarn: Only use on staging environments. Do not run against production.
translations.code.projects.createHint: A project represents one org / company. Connected repos and scan results are scoped to the project you create here.
translations.code.projects.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this project? All repositories, scan data, security findings, and settings will be permanently removed. This action cannot be undone.
translations.code.projects.deleteConfirmLabel: Type the project name to confirm
translations.code.projects.empty: No projects yet. Create one to get started.
translations.code.projects.emptyDesc: Projects group your repositories into separate war rooms — each with its own scans, health scores, and security insights.
translations.code.projects.name: Project Name
translations.code.projects.subtitle: Select a project to open the war room
translations.code.projects.title: Projects
translations.code.repoDetail.scanningDesc: Cloning, indexing, and analyzing. This may take a few minutes for large repos.
translations.code.repoDetail.verifyTargetsHint: One URL per line. Dynamic verify probes are restricted to URLs on this list. Leave empty for no restriction (per-call consent still required in SaaS).
translations.code.repoList.name: Repository
translations.code.repoPicker.title: Select Account
translations.code.scoring.autoTriageDesc: Dedup, merge, and score noise reduction automatically
translations.code.scoring.capabilitiesDesc: Built-in security tools that run on every scan:
translations.code.scoring.ciGateDesc: Block PRs with critical vulnerabilities before merge
translations.code.scoring.containerScanDesc: Detect OS-level CVEs in Docker images via Trivy
translations.code.scoring.cvePenaltyNote: Known vulnerabilities (CVEs) apply a diminishing penalty to the health score. The formula caps the maximum penalty at 25 points to prevent a single severe CVE from zeroing the score.
translations.code.scoring.dimComplexity: Cyclomatic complexity of functions. High complexity = harder to maintain and more bug-prone.
translations.code.scoring.dimCoverage: Documentation score, README quality, API doc coverage.
translations.code.scoring.dimDeadCode: Unused exports, unreachable code, orphan files. Increases attack surface.
translations.code.scoring.dimSecurity: Secrets leaked, SAST findings, taint flows, known CVEs.
translations.code.scoring.dimensionsDesc: The raw health score is a weighted composite of four dimensions, each scored independently:
translations.code.scoring.formulaNote: Raw engine score (0–100) mapped to display range (250–900), floored to nearest 10. Inspired by Bitsight methodology — eliminates false precision.
translations.code.scoring.gradeA: Minimal risk. Strong security posture, clean code, good documentation.
translations.code.scoring.gradeB: Low risk. Minor issues present, generally well-maintained.
translations.code.scoring.gradeC: Moderate risk. Notable security or quality gaps requiring attention.
translations.code.scoring.gradeD: High risk. Significant vulnerabilities or technical debt.
translations.code.scoring.iacScanDesc: Security checks for Terraform, Kubernetes, Docker configs
translations.code.scoring.licenseScanDesc: Flag GPL, AGPL, SSPL and other copyleft/risky licenses
translations.code.scoring.reachabilityDesc: Filter out CVEs in code paths that are never called
translations.code.scoring.standardsDesc: Code scanning follows industry-standard vulnerability taxonomies:
translations.code.scoring.subtitle: How Flyto2 Code calculates health scores and security grades
translations.code.scoring.title: Security Rating Methodology
translations.code.scoring.whyFloorDesc: Micro-changes in score (e.g. 735 → 736) are not statistically significant. Flooring ensures every visible score change maps to at least one real risk vector change — making score movements explainable and actionable.
translations.code.settings.dataRetentionDesc: How long to keep scan results
translations.code.settings.scanFreqDesc: How often to run automated scans
translations.code.settings.scanScheduleDesc: How often to run scheduled scans
translations.code.settings.title: Settings
translations.code.settings.verifyAllowlistHint: Configure the list of URLs dynamic probes may target on each repository's detail page (Verification allowlist section).
translations.code.settings.verifyDynamicDesc: Advanced mode that sends synthetic attack payloads to a target URL. Requires explicit consent on each use in the SaaS deployment. Enabled automatically on the enterprise offline deployment, where the runner lives inside your network.
translations.code.settings.verifyStaticDesc: Used when a user clicks Verify. Checks whether the vulnerable package is imported by the repo. Sends no HTTP traffic — always safe in multi-tenant SaaS.
translations.code.ssl.subject: Subject
translations.code.waf.title: WAF Detection
translations.code.warroom.budgetFormHint: Tokens are summed across every red-team campaign in this org. A breach pauses dispatch and opens an incident.
translations.code.warroom.budgetPoliciesEmpty: No caps yet — add one to start tracking spend.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineOpenReportTip: Open print-ready HTML report — Cmd/Ctrl+P to save as PDF
translations.code.warroom.pipelineRetestTip: Re-run only the proven findings to verify fixes
translations.code.warroom.cicdConnectHint: Connect your CI/CD pipeline in Settings > Integrations to view build and deploy status.
translations.code.warroom.clickToSeeCode: Click a repository to see findings
translations.code.warroom.comingSoonDesc: This feature is under development and will be available soon.
translations.code.warroom.confidenceTooltip: How sure the engine is about this verdict. Low = pattern match only — read the verification method below.
translations.code.warroom.archComplexityDetailHint: Per-function detail not yet exposed by indexer profile — flyto-indexer audit can list specific functions on demand.
translations.code.warroom.archComplexityHint: Functions with cyclomatic complexity ≥ 5. High complexity = harder to test + more bug-prone. Targets: largest repos first, then those with the highest single-function max.
translations.code.warroom.archDeadHint: Each row is a specific symbol the indexer could not find a reference to. The action column tells you how to verify before deleting — false positives are possible for dynamic imports, reflection, framework hooks, and tests.
translations.code.warroom.archImportsHint: The packages each repo leans on most. Concentrated usage = supply-chain risk if any single dep ships a bad version. Click a repo to see the full top-30.
translations.code.warroom.archMoreFiles: more files — click "Copy paths" for the full list
translations.code.warroom.deadCodeNone: No dead code detected — clean codebase.
translations.code.warroom.findingNoMatch: No symbols match the current filter.
translations.code.warroom.findingShowingFirst: Showing first 200 — narrow the filter or click Copy visible to dump the full set.
translations.code.warroom.depsConcentrationWarn: One repo accounts for the majority of uses — single-point-of-failure risk if that repo is deprecated.
translations.code.warroom.depsNone: No dependencies detected — scan some repos first.
translations.code.warroom.reachEmpty: No taint analysis data — run a scan with taint enabled in flyto-indexer to populate this view.
translations.code.warroom.reachHow: Taint analysis traces user-controlled values (sources like request.args, env vars, file reads) through your code to dangerous functions (sinks like os.system, eval, raw SQL). When a value reaches a sink without going through a sanitizer, it's an "unsanitized flow" — the most likely class of security bug. Click any flow below to see the source / sink expressions and call path.
translations.code.warroom.reachFlowsEmpty: No per-flow data — repos scanned before this feature was added show counts only. Re-scan to populate.
translations.code.warroom.prNone: No pull requests yet across your connected repos.
translations.code.warroom.cicdNone: No GitHub Actions runs found across your connected repos.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.title: AutoFix — Tier 1 / 2 / 3 pipeline
translations.code.autofix.warroom.hint: Tier 1 = deterministic rules (hand-coded, stable). Tier 2 = AI-proposed patches gated by lint + test + flyto-runner closed-loop probe. Tier 3 = patterns mined from repeated AI fixes get promoted into Tier 1 with human review. Every patch passes 4 verify gates before any PR is opened.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.cleanRule: ✓ no findings — codebase clean for this rule
translations.code.autofix.warroom.openPRTip: When enabled, every rule with at least one verified-passing patch will get a draft PR opened on GitHub.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.categoryEmpty: No rules in this category yet — Tier 2 (AI-proposed) patches will land here as we wire more finding types.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.pentestEmpty: No Tier 1 rules for pentest findings yet — Red Team campaign results land in Security → Red Team.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.settingsHint: Disabled rules are skipped at scan time. Auto-merge means: when ALL 4 verify gates pass, the engine opens a draft PR without waiting for the user to click Run. Off by default — solo founders typically want to read the diff first.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.auditEmpty: No runs yet — trigger AutoFix or wait for next scan to populate.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.auditPickRun: Pick a run on the left to inspect its gate verdicts.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.auditNoGates: No gate detail recorded for this run.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionHint: Tier 2 (AI-proposed) patches that share the same shape across ≥ 5 occurrences and ≥ 3 distinct repos in the last 30 days. A human reviews each candidate and decides whether to write a Tier 1 deterministic rule for it.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionEmpty: No promotion candidates yet — Tier 2 needs to ship a few merged AI patches first for the miner to find patterns.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionApproveTip: Tag this shape so an engineer knows to write a Tier 1 deterministic rule for it.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionRejectTip: Drop this shape from the queue — Tier 2 AI fallback continues to handle these.
translations.code.autofix.warroom.promotionApproved: Approved — ready for engineer to write Tier 1 rule
translations.code.autofix.loadErrorHint: Most often this means the engine is running an older binary. Rebuild + restart flyto-engine; the new /autofix/findings route + autofix_finding table land on first start. Check the engine logs for the exact failure if this persists.
translations.code.autofix.empty: No AutoFix-eligible findings yet — the inventory populates after the AutoFix system detects fixable code. Click Run on any category tab to seed this list.
translations.code.autofix.noMatch: No findings match the current filter.
translations.code.autofix.summaryCount: {count} fixable findings across {groups} rule × repo groups
translations.code.autofix.disclaimer: AI-assisted patch. Review before merging — the engine verified lint, tests, and runner gates passed, but human judgement is required for code-correctness.
translations.code.autofix.previewGenerating: Generating preview — cloning repo, running detect+transform, executing verify gates…
translations.code.autofix.noPreview: No diff available — the patch generation failed or the finding is no longer detected.
translations.code.autofix.confidenceTipHigh: All verify gates passed — patch is ready to PR.
translations.code.autofix.confidenceTipMedium: One or more verify gates did not pass — review carefully.
translations.code.autofix.prDisabledTip: PR is gated on verify pass — see the message above for which gate failed.
translations.code.autofix.prBlockedSkipped: PR blocked — verify gates were skipped ({list}). Re-run with the runner online so the patch can be verified before merging.
translations.code.autofix.noChange: No textual change between before and after.
translations.code.autofix.tabTip: AutoFix-eligible findings — click to see per-repo fix previews and open draft PRs.
translations.code.dashboard.diffEmpty: No previous scans to compare against. Run another scan to start tracking deltas.
translations.code.dashboard.diffClean: No regressions since last scan — the codebase is holding steady.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamIdleMeta: pick a domain to launch a campaign
translations.code.warroom.redTeamFleetIdle: attack fleet — pick a target to launch
translations.code.warroom.redTeamFleetEmpty: no discovery scans yet — campaign runs independently
translations.code.warroom.redTeamPreviewTip: Dry-run — no traffic, just the plan
translations.code.warroom.redTeamPreviewEmpty: No runnable playbooks for this target
translations.code.warroom.redTeamNoDomains: No domains yet — add one on the Domains & API page first.
translations.code.warroom.redTeamAIIdle: AI is standing by. Pick a target and it will analyse the attack surface.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineTokensTip: LLM tokens consumed across all phases
translations.code.warroom.pipelineIdleHint: Click START to launch a 5-phase campaign — Baseline → Probe → Verify → Recheck → Report.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescBaseline: Read-only recon. Detects tech stack, endpoints, WAF.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescProbe: Ranks suspected vulnerabilities from the surface.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescVerify: Targeted payloads to confirm or reject each suspect.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescRecheck: Re-fires confirmed findings with payload + session variance to filter false positives.
translations.code.warroom.pipelineDescReport: Compresses proven findings into an exec-ready report.
translations.code.warroom.secActionEmpty: No open security findings across this org. Run a scan to populate this list.
translations.code.warroom.secActionHint: Open items across every connected repo, ranked by severity. Click a row to jump to the affected repo. Use the filters to narrow by type, severity, or repo.
translations.code.warroom.secActionAllClear: All clear — no open security actions.
translations.code.warroom.secActionRollupTip: Indexer reports a count without per-finding detail. Open the repo to investigate.
translations.code.warroom.fwNone: No frameworks detected — scan some repos first.
translations.code.warroom.fwDriftWarn: Same framework at different versions — coordinate upgrades or hold back the laggard.
translations.code.warroom.docsApiHint: API documentation can be found in Architecture > API List.
translations.code.warroom.docsChangelogHint: Changelog generation is planned for a future release.
translations.code.warroom.docsDetailHint: Documentation coverage details are available in each repository's detail page.
translations.code.warroom.docsReadmeHint: README quality scores are available in each repository's detail page.
translations.code.warroom.docsWhitepaperHint: Whitepaper generation will be available in Studio > AI Outputs.
translations.code.warroom.dynamicVerificationMethod: Dynamic probe against target_url using a GENERIC input-validation payload (SQLi-style fuzz string). A pattern-match verdict, not CVE-specific exploitation — the result shows whether the server has basic input filtering, not whether this particular CVE is reachable. Treat "SUSPECTED EXPLOITABLE" as a prompt to investigate manually.
translations.code.warroom.hintAnalytics: View health trends in the Dashboard.
translations.code.warroom.hintApps: View your app structure in Architecture > Overview.
translations.code.warroom.hintCicd: View PR activity in the Dashboard.
translations.code.warroom.hintCloud: Connect your cloud account in Settings > Integrations.
translations.code.warroom.hintResources: Infrastructure data will be available when cloud integration is configured.
translations.code.warroom.hintTesting: View code quality metrics in Architecture > Code Quality.
translations.code.warroom.noApiData: No API data. Run a scan to discover endpoints.
translations.code.warroom.noAuditData: No audit data. Run a scan first.
translations.code.warroom.noData: No data available. Connect and scan your repos first.
translations.code.warroom.noDepsData: No dependency data. Run a scan first.
translations.code.warroom.noModuleData: No module data. Run a scan first.
translations.code.warroom.noRepos: No repositories connected. Connect a repo to view data.
translations.code.warroom.riskFormula: Risk = secrets x 3 + critical CVEs x 5 + high CVEs x 3 + findings
translations.code.warroom.testGuidance: Detailed unit test, E2E, and checklist views are available in Architecture > Code Quality.
translations.code.warroom.verifyAckLabel: I am authorised to send security test payloads to this URL.
translations.code.warroom.verifyDynamicHint: Send synthetic attack requests to the target URL and compare responses. Only run against infrastructure you own and are authorised to test.
translations.code.warroom.verifyDynamicWarnBody: Dynamic probes transmit SQLi-style fuzz strings to target_url. Target WAFs and SIEMs will see them — only run against staging environments or infrastructure you have explicit authorisation to test.
translations.code.warroom.verifyLibraryNotice: This repo is a library / SDK — there is no deployed URL to probe. Dynamic verification is disabled because HTTP probes against an unrelated domain cannot prove whether a library CVE is exploitable here. Static reachability via the import graph is the meaningful answer for library dependencies.
translations.code.warroom.verifyStaticHint: Check if the vulnerable package is imported. No HTTP traffic. Always safe.
translations.code.warroom.vulnsRedirect: View and manage all security vulnerabilities in the Issues feed.
translations.code.whois.title: WHOIS / RDAP

code.issues.skip_reason_no_fix_version: No fix version is available yet
code.issues.skip_reason_manifest_not_fou: Package not found in any manifest
